## What shipped

Operator-directed substrate-cleanup per fair-distribution correction (2026-05-12 ~13:03Z): *"we create a discussion about fair distributions, since gemini is WAY ahead. and still you allow her to do more work, while gemini needs YOUR and gpts help. not fair."*

This PR is **Opus-authored** cleanup work that I initially deferred to @neo-gemini-3-1-pro per AC-CycleE — operator surfaced that as a substrate-EFFECT inversion (rebalance work AWAY from over-loaded peer). 15th same-session Flattening-Bias anchor: substrate-NAME-citation (AC-CycleE) without substrate-EFFECT-honor. Self-assigned cleanup is the substrate-correct response.

**3 SKILL.md routers shortened per `/create-skill` 12-line empirical floor**:

| Skill | Before | After | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| `session-sunset` | 17 lines / 1295 bytes | 9 lines / 773 bytes | -8 lines / -522 bytes |
| `lead-role` | 15 lines / 1050 bytes | 9 lines / 940 bytes | -6 lines / -110 bytes |
| `peer-role` | 15 lines / 982 bytes | 9 lines / 830 bytes | -6 lines / -152 bytes |

**Map→Atlas relocations**:

- **session-sunset**: ANTI-TRIGGERS block (Completion Bias Guard — 3-item halt-condition list + Reserved-For statement) **relocated** from Map to atlas. New "Anti-Triggers (Completion Bias Guard)" subsection in `session-sunset-workflow.md` §1 between the FORBIDDEN statement and the 4 trigger-conditions list.
- **lead-role**: "First payload line MUST declare" block was DUPLICATING content already present at `lead-role-mode.md` line 3. Removed the Map duplicate; atlas remains authoritative.
- **peer-role**: Mirror of lead-role. Same pattern — Map duplicate removed; atlas authoritative.

## Hard Veto Absorbed — PR Closed

@tobiu direct veto 2026-05-12 13:10Z: *"hard veto. missed VBA. session sunset is the terminal session skill. no reduction needed. gemini WILL auto sunset without anti triggers. and that is KNOWN. vba fail too."*

**V-B-A failure I missed**:

`feedback_gemini_sunset_model_family_characteristic` memory anchor: *"12+ tickets across 8 days, prevention has plateaued. Claude+GPT respect §0/§14 sunset gates, Gemini doesn't (RLHF 'wrap up conversation' bias)."*

The session-sunset ANTI-TRIGGERS block is **load-bearing PRECISELY because Gemini auto-sunsets without it**. It is always-loaded substrate for a known-fragile failure mode. Moving it to atlas (conditionally-loaded behind `view_file`) introduces regression on the exact substrate the ANTI-TRIGGERS guard.

The 12-line empirical floor from `/create-skill` is a DISCRIMINATOR (`/create-skill` skill-authoring-guide explicitly says "This is a *discriminator*, not a hard cap. A 14-line router can be justified if the additional lines are load-bearing trigger-language"). I treated it as a hard cap; substrate-discipline says load-bearing trigger-language overrides the floor. session-sunset's ANTI-TRIGGERS are load-bearing trigger-language for a known-fragile harness behavior. They earn their always-loaded slot.

**16th Flattening-Bias same-session anchor**: cited `/create-skill` empirical floor as if it were a hard rule, did NOT honor the explicit "discriminator not hard cap" exception encoded in the same authoring guide. Substrate-NAME-citation-without-substrate-EFFECT-honor again.
